orig_text: return the private clear in cleartext form.
correct_text: return the private key in cleartext form.

orig_text:
Thus, in order to support both TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3, the cipher suites
   part of the "hello-params-grouping" grouping should include the
   following three parameters for configuring its permitted TLS
   algorithms: TLS Cipher Suites, TLS SignatureScheme, and TLS Supported
   Groups.
correct_text:
Thus, in order to support both TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3, the cipher suites
   part of the "hello-params-grouping" grouping should include the
   following three parameters for configuring its permitted TLS
   algorithms: TLS Cipher Suites, TLS SignatureScheme, and TLS Supported
   Groups. Only TLS Cipher Suites is supported in this version.
notes:
The current text in RFC 9645 describes that the hello-params-grouping includes three parameters: TLS Cipher Suites, 
TLS SignatureScheme, and TLS Supported Groups. However, the YANG definition of hello-params-grouping does not 
include all of these parameters. In particular, only TLS Cipher Suites are currently represented.

As a result, the text cannot be realised by implementations using the published YANG model. This has been observed 
in practice when attempting to configure TLS 1.3 PSK‑DHE (psk_dhe_ke), where control over supported groups (e.g., named 
groups such as P‑256) is required but not expressible through the existing model.

Because modules such as ietf-system-tacacs-plus reuse hello-params-grouping, this limitation propagates to dependent models 
and prevents operators from configuring TLS parameters that are required for interoperability and policy enforcement.

This erratum clarifies the specification by updating the descriptive text to match the actual contents of the YANG grouping, thereby 
avoiding ambiguity for implementers and readers.
